title: Nanoparticles and Surfactants at Liquid-Liquid Interfaces

content.text: University of Bremen, Hybrid Materials Interfaces Group (Feb 2022 - Jan 2025)

content.html:
Methods and Scientific Focus

This work explores how functionalized silica nanoparticles and surface-active molecules interact at oil-water interfaces, using molecular simulation, continuum electrostatics, and synchrotron-based interfacial characterization. The emphasis is on mechanisms, methodology, and interfacial physics.

items: Interfacial thermodynamics: liquid-liquid interfaces, interfacial tension, surface pressure, contact angle, particle wettability, and adsorption energetics.
Colloidal and electrostatic interactions: electric double layers, Debye screening, counterion structuring, surfactant packing, charge regulation, and competitive adsorption.
Nanoparticle functionalization: APTES-modified silica surfaces, protonation behavior, and pH-dependent charge states.
Surfactant organization at interfaces: monolayer formation, exclusion/depletion zones near charged objects, and coupling between molecular ordering and interfacial stress.

content.html:
Methods and Techniques Demonstrated

1. Classical Molecular Dynamics (MD)

items: Construction of multiphase simulation boxes (water/oil/surfactant/salt).
Modeling functionalized nanoparticles with grafted organic groups.
Handling protonation states, counterion neutralization, and charge balance.
Long-timescale simulations (hundreds of nanoseconds).
Analysis of density profiles, radial distribution functions (RDF and 2D-RDF), interfacial tension via pressure tensor, nanoparticle position, contact angle, and stability.
Use of Poisson-Boltzmann theory to model electrostatic potentials.
Analytical and numerical solutions (APBS).
Treatment of heterogeneous dielectric environments (water versus oil).
Linking continuum predictions to atomistic simulation behavior.
Structural characterization of interfacial layers.
Extraction of particle coverage, interfacial electron density profiles, and surfactant layer organization.
Comparison of interfacial behavior under varying salt and surfactant conditions.
ESRF Grenoble attendance (01.03.2022 - 06.03.2022).
DESY PETRA III attendance (26.10.2022 - 31.10.2022).
Active participation in the experimental group for GISAXS/GISACS and X-ray measurements.
Pendant-drop measurements.
Quantification of surfactant adsorption via surface pressure.
Use of interfacial tension trends to infer molecular organization.

Temperature-Dependent Behavior of C14 - C18 Alkyl Surfactants with Ionizable and Neutral Head Groups at the Water-Decane Interface,
F. Menke, S. Amiri, K. Rezwan, L. C. Ciacchi, M. Maas, Submitted. 2025

Coated Silica Nanoparticles and Surfactant at the Water-Oil Interface
S. Amiri, F. Menke, M. Maas, L. C. Ciacchi, Under preparation.

Particle-Based Materials Symposium (Talk),
Sep 2024, Bremen.
GROMACS for MD simulations.
VMD for molecular visualization.
MDAnalysis for trajectory loading and processing.
GNU Parallel for large-scale batch analysis.
APBS for electrostatic analysis.
All calculations and simulations executed on HPC clusters (HLRN via Slurm).
All pre- and post-processing steps scripted in Python and Bash.
Integration of simulation, theory, and experiment into one reproducible workflow.
